title: Dynamic Alignment: A Fragile Survival Effect
summary:
arXiv:2605.11305v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Dynamic alignment in magnetohydrodynamic (MHD) turbulence is usually interpreted as a cascade-wide tendency of Elsasser increments to become increasingly collinear at smaller scales. We argue instead that the standard measurements mainly detect a conditional survival effect of intense events. In high-resolution Johns Hopkins MHD simulations, the typical folded Elsasser-increment angle remains only modestly below the random-orientation baseline and shows no evidence for a rigid, monotone, volume-filling ordering of the cascade.
